datasets_used: 70,000 compositions from Ti-Fe-Al-V-Ni-Nb-Zr-Mn-Co design space, labeled with phase fractions at 7 temperatures using CALPHAD
headline_result: Laves phase R² of 0.99 on 70,000 composition dataset
ablations: Feature reduction from 41 to 12 most important features preserved accuracy; further reduction caused accuracy drops and increased epistemic uncertainty
improvement_over_baseline: Low-uncertainty route increased BCC fraction by 32% (0.68→0.90) vs high-uncertainty route; high-uncertainty route achieved 0.75+ precision vs low-uncertainty route's ~0.30 F1
stated_limitations: The paper addresses limitations of existing machine learning approaches that rely on deterministic mappings and neglect uncertainty in predictions
unstated_weaknesses: The paper doesn't explicitly address noisy labels which is a key concern in the project
Domain specificity to materials science may limit direct applicability to medical data
Unclear performance with very small datasets
threats_to_validity: Domain specificity may limit generalization to medical tabular data
Uncertainty quantification focuses on aleatoric uncertainty but may not fully address epistemic uncertainty
Lack of explicit handling of noisy labels
reproducibility: medium (code availability not mentioned, deep learning models often require specific architectures and hyperparameters)
open_questions: How does the MDN approach perform with very small datasets?
Can the uncertainty quantification methods be effectively used for calibration?
How does the approach handle noisy labels?
gaps_this_enables: Provides a baseline for uncertainty-aware learning in small datasets
MDN approach could be adapted for medical tabular data
Active learning framework could be applied to medical data discovery
relevance_to_topic: The paper addresses uncertainty-aware learning and active learning but in a different domain and without explicit focus on calibration or noisy labels
reusable_assets: Active learning framework for out-of-domain discovery
best_result: Laves phase R² of 0.99 on 70,000 composition dataset
limitations_stated: The paper addresses limitations of existing machine learning approaches that rely on deterministic mappings and neglect uncertainty in predictions
title: Uncertainty-aware phase fraction prediction and active-learning-guided out-of-domain discovery of refractory multi-principal element alloys
